analysis.semantic_summary: Parser designed to process Nginx error logs, extracting timestamp, severity, process ID, thread ID, connection ID and message text. Specifically handles the standard Nginx error log format with support for multi-line entries.
optimization_opportunities: Add error handling for malformed logs
analysis.lua_code:
-- SentinelOne Parser: nginx_error_logs-latest 
-- OCSF Class: System Activity (1001)
-- Performance Level: High-volume optimized
-- Generated: 2025-10-13T12:24:31.517073

-- Pre-compile patterns for performance
local TIMESTAMP_PATTERN = "^(%d%d%d%d)/(%d%d)/(%d%d) (%d%d):(%d%d):(%d%d)"
local SEVERITY_LEVELS = {
    emerg = 1,
    alert = 2, 
    crit = 3,
    error = 4,
    warn = 5,
    notice = 6,
    info = 7,
    debug = 8
}

function transform(record)
    -- Input validation with early return
    if not record or type(record) ~= "table" then
        return nil, "Invalid input record"
    end

    -- Initialize local variables for performance
    local output = {
        class_uid = 1001,
        class_name = "System Activity", 
        category_uid = 1,
        category_name = "System",
        activity_id = 1,
        type_uid = 100101,
        metadata = {
            product = {
                name = "Nginx Web Server",
                vendor = "Nginx"
            },
            version = "1.0"
        }
    }

    -- Process timestamp with error handling
    if record.timestamp then
        local year, month, day, hour, min, sec = record.timestamp:match(TIMESTAMP_PATTERN)
        if year then
            -- Convert to UNIX timestamp (milliseconds)
            output.time = os.time({
                year = tonumber(year),
                month = tonumber(month), 
                day = tonumber(day),
                hour = tonumber(hour),
                min = tonumber(min),
                sec = tonumber(sec)
            }) * 1000
        end
    end

    -- Map severity with normalization
    if record.severity then
        local sev = record.severity:lower()
        output.severity = SEVERITY_LEVELS[sev] or 0
        output.severity_txt = sev
    end

    -- Process IDs with type validation
    if record.pid then
        local pid = tonumber(record.pid)
        if pid then
            if not output.process then output.process = {} end
            output.process.pid = pid
        end
    end

    if record.tid then
        local tid = tonumber(record.tid)
        if tid then
            if not output.thread then output.thread = {} end
            output.thread.id = tid
        end
    end

    -- Handle message text with sanitization
    if record.text then
        output.message = string.gsub(record.text, "[^%g%s]", "") -- Remove control chars
    end

    -- Connection ID handling
    if record.cid then
        if not output.connection then output.connection = {} end
        output.connection.uid = tostring(record.cid)
    end

    -- Validation and enrichment
    if not output.time then
        output.time = os.time() * 1000
    end

    -- Add observability metadata
    output.observability = {
        parser_id = "nginx_error_logs-latest",
        ingestion_time = os.time() * 1000
    }

    return output
end

-- Error wrapper for production use
function safe_transform(record)
    local status, result = pcall(transform, record)
    if status then
        return result
    else
        return nil, string.format("Transform error: %s", result)
    end
end

-- Optimization notes:
-- 1. Pre-compiled patterns improve regex performance
-- 2. Local variable usage optimizes memory access
-- 3. Table pre-allocation reduces memory churn
-- 4. Early validation prevents unnecessary processing
-- 5. Error handling prevents runtime crashes

deployment_notes.monitoring_setup: Enable performance tracking via Observo metrics API
